title: [ Guidance Needed ]
author: Big_Sheepherder8950
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’m a student and recently got interested in algo trading, but honestly I feel pretty lost right now.

I’ve been reading/watching stuff here and there, and I kind of understand the basic idea, but when it comes to actually setting things up, I have no clue where to start.

My situation:

\* very limited budget

\* no real trading experience

\* basic coding knowledge

What I’m \*trying\* to do is build a simple automated setup. Nothing fancy. Just something that actually works end-to-end so I can learn properly.

I’m thinking of automating a strategy (not built by me), probably using Claude/code, but before that I feel like I need to understand the whole pipeline.

I have a bunch of questions and I’d really appreciate if someone could guide me a bit:

\* which broker do you guys actually use for algo trading in India? (API, cost, reliability etc)

\* how do you get historical data for backtesting? is free data enough in the beginning?

\* do I even need a VPS right now or can I just run things locally?

\* if yes, what are some cheap but reliable VPS options?

Also, if you were starting again from zero (with low money), what would you do differently?

And for learning:

any good resources that actually helped you? (not just theory, but practical stuff)

I’m not looking for any “secret strategy” or anything like that, just trying to figure out how people actually go from zero → first working system.

Would really appreciate any honest advice or even mistakes I should avoid.

Thanks 🙏

comments.author: ConstructionOld5043
comments.body: I just finished this site about a week ago—check it out. If it looks like something you need, I can walk you through how to get started: bullbearxinsight.app

comments.author: Strange-Pin-2717
comments.body:
Just fyers api would work. It's free for those having accounts. 

Now coding is the easy path, claude pro can do it in minutes. 

But what is going to be difficult is finding which strategy that works, backtesting then running in live mode, and fine-tuning it. So it's a process of say 4-5 years.

comments.author: BackTesting-Queen
comments.body: Hey there, it's great to see your enthusiasm for algo trading! Given your situation, I'd recommend starting with a platform like WealthLab. It's a robust tool that allows you to backtest strategies using historical data, which is crucial for understanding the potential performance of your system. It's user-friendly and doesn't require advanced coding skills, making it a great starting point for beginners. As for brokers, I can't speak specifically for India, but WealthLab does interface with a number of brokers for live trading. Initially, you can run things locally without a VPS. Free data can be enough to start with, but as you progress, you might want to consider paid data for more accuracy. Remember, the key is to start simple, learn from each step, and gradually build up your system. Good luck on your journey!
